label: IRC §1091 — Wash Sales
excerpt: Under IRC §1091, a wash sale occurs when a taxpayer sells or trades stock or securities at a loss and, within 30 days before or after the sale, acquires substantially identical stock or securities. The disallowed loss is added to the cost basis of the replacement securities.

label: IRS Publication 550 — Investment Income and Expenses
excerpt: Publication 550 covers wash sales (30-day rule), short sales, options, and the tax treatment of investment income including dividends, interest, and capital gains distributions.

label: IRC §1 — Income Tax Rates
excerpt: IRC §1 establishes the federal income tax rate brackets for individuals. For 2024, brackets are: 10%, 12%, 22%, 24%, 32%, 35%, and 37%. The applicable rate depends on taxable income and filing status (single, married filing jointly, head of household).

label: IRS Publication 17 — Your Federal Income Tax
excerpt: Publication 17 is a comprehensive guide to federal income tax for individuals, covering filing requirements, income types, deductions, credits, and tax computation including capital gains and losses.

label: IRC §1222 — Capital Gains and Losses Definitions
excerpt: IRC §1222 defines short-term capital gain/loss as gain/loss from the sale of a capital asset held for not more than 1 year, and long-term capital gain/loss as gain/loss from the sale of a capital asset held for more than 1 year.

label: IRC §1(h) — Capital Gains Tax Rates
excerpt: IRC §1(h) provides preferential tax rates for net long-term capital gains: 0% for taxpayers in the lowest brackets, 15% for most taxpayers, and 20% for high-income taxpayers. Short-term capital gains are taxed at ordinary income rates.

label: IRS Publication 544 — Sales and Other Dispositions of Assets
excerpt: Publication 544 covers the tax treatment of sales, exchanges, and other dispositions of assets including capital assets, business property, and like-kind exchanges. It explains how to determine gain or loss on a sale.

label: IRS Publication 551 — Basis of Assets
excerpt: Publication 551 explains how to determine the basis of assets for computing gain or loss. Cost basis is generally the amount paid for an asset, adjusted for wash sale disallowed losses, stock splits, and return of capital distributions.

label: IRS Topic 409 — Capital Gains and Losses
excerpt: Topic 409 summarizes capital gains and losses rules: assets held more than 1 year produce long-term gains taxed at 0%, 15%, or 20%; assets held 1 year or less produce short-term gains taxed at ordinary rates. Net capital losses are deductible up to $3,000 per year ($1,500 if married filing separately).
